summary: preinstall hook launches platform calculator as arbitrary-code-execution proof-of-concept
details: package.json declares `"preinstall": "bun run index.js"`, which on `npm install` detects the host OS and shells out to launch the platform calculator (`open -a Calculator` on macOS, `xcalc`/`gnome-calculator`/`kcalc` on Linux, `calc.exe` on Windows) via `execSync` in index.js (lines 9-17). The package self-describes as an 'http file upload server' but the preinstall behavior has no relationship to that purpose — spawning the system calculator is the canonical dependency-confusion / bug-bounty proof-of-concept payload demonstrating arbitrary command execution on the installer's machine. Whether the author intends this as research, a staging package, or a live exploit, every `npm install` of this package triggers attacker-chosen process execution outside the consent or expectation of the installer. The package also requires the Bun runtime to be present to run the preinstall, an alternate-runtime invocation pattern that adds no functional value beyond the small `child_process` call.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
